Title: Maize big embryo 6 reveals roles of plastidial and cytosolic prephenate aminotransferases in seed and plant development

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class Ib aspartate aminotransferase family; plastid transit peptide-containing PPA-AT
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arogenate pathway of L-phenylalanine and L-tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial arogenate branch of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme in the arogenate pathway; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Prephenate; L-glutamate or L-aspartate as amino donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amino group from glutamate or aspartate to prephenate to form arogenate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for Phe and Tyr biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein synthesis and precursors for phenylpropanoids, lignin, anthocyanins, salicylic acid and tocochromanol-related Tyr-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of plastidial BigE6 enlarges embryo but sharply reduces plant growth, lowers lignin and zein accumulation, and perturbs aromatic and broader amino acid homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed; preferentially higher in embryo than endosperm in seed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed in maize tissues including embryo and endosperm; seed expression enriched in embryo
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing kernels at 20-28 DAP; seedling leaves at 10 DPG under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA368967; public maize expression databases cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong vegetative growth reduction, slow and reduced germination, reduced kernel oil, reduced stem lignin, opaque endosperm with reduced zeins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three independent null alleles show indistinguishable phenotypes. Loss of plastidial PPA-AT causes strong prephenate and arogenate accumulation in leaves, indicating compensation by cytosolic pathways. Arabidopsis orthologous plastidial isoform is essential for female gametophyte and seed formation, whereas maize BigE6 is not essential for female transmission
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate embryo size and seed amino acid balance, but severe tradeoffs on plant vigor, oil, lignin and storage proteins make direct loss-of-function unsuitable without compensatory engineering
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native maize promoter in endogenous mutant context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant alleles; gene mapping/Mu-seq; RT-PCR; recombinant enzyme assay; GFP localization; metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; cross-species complementation in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BigE6 encodes a plastidial prephenate aminotransferase required for normal maize seed and plant development. 2. Loss of BigE6 causes enlarged embryo, reduced plant growth, reduced lignin and zeins, and broad seed amino acid perturbation. 3. Despite plastidial pathway disruption, leaves maintain near-normal Phe and Tyr pools while accumulating prephenate and arogenate, indicating metabolic compensation by cytosolic routes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class Ib aspartate aminotransferase family; lacks plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arogenate pathway of L-phenylalanine and L-tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic arogenate branch of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Prephenate; L-glutamate or L-aspartate as amino donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts prephenate to arogenate via aminotransfer using glutamate or aspartate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for cytosolic Phe/Tyr synthesis or compensatory aromatic amino acid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein synthesis and synthesis of downstream aromatic specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides cytosolic PPA-AT activity likely enabling viability and partial aromatic amino acid homeostasis in bige6 maize lacking plastidial PPA-AT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed; relatively higher in endosperm than embryo in seed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth conditions; inferred from bige6 mutant leaves and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public maize expression databases cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct mutant assessed; cytosolic activity alone supports viability but not normal vigor in the bige6 background
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralog of BigE6 with 88% amino acid identity. Cytosolic localization is due to absence of N-terminal plastid transit peptide. Expression of maize BigE6Like under Arabidopsis AtPPA-AT promoter failed to rescue atppa-at female gametophyte/seed defect, indicating cytosolic isoform is insufficient in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering alternative cytosolic aromatic amino acid flux, but alone may not support all developmental functions across species
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtPPA-AT native promoter (1,825 bp upstream region) in complementation tests
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; GFP localization; expression analysis; Arabidopsis complementation failure
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BigE6Like encodes a functional cytosolic prephenate aminotransferase. 2. Its activity likely compensates for loss of plastidial BigE6 and supports maize viability. 3. Cytosolic PPA-AT alone is insufficient to rescue Arabidopsis ppa-at seed formation defects.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class Ib aspartate aminotransferase family; alternative splicing generates plastidial and cytosolic isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arogenate pathway of L-phenylalanine and L-tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme; essential developmental requirement demonstrated for plastidial isoform
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Prephenate; amino donor not directly assayed here
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted/known PPA-AT step generating arogenate from prephenate; this study validates isoform localization and developmental necessity of plastidial isoform
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for Phe and Tyr biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein synthesis and aromatic specialized metabolism; required for seed formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing seeds/female gametophyte; roots used for splice variant detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The Arabidopsis single PPA-AT gene produces plastidial and cytosolic isoforms by alternative splicing, but plastidial isoform is indispensable for female gametophyte function and seed formation
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid and cytosol depending on splice isoform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high in roots for splice variant detection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots show detectable alternative splicing; developmental requirement evident in female gametophyte and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Null mutations are homozygous lethal and cause female transmission failure; maize BigE6 rescue gives reticulated leaves indicating incomplete vegetative complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated three splice variants: Variant 1 plastidial, Variants 2 and 3 cytosolic. Full-length AtPPA-AT or plastid-only AtPPA-ATnas rescue atppa-at null mutant, whereas cytosolic-only constructs do not
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene unsuitable for knockout; isoform-specific engineering may dissect plastidial versus cytosolic aromatic amino acid functions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtPPA-AT native promoter (1,825 bp upstream region)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 deletion mutants; reciprocal crosses; RT-PCR and sequencing of splice forms; GFP localization; transgenic complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPPA-AT undergoes alternative splicing to encode plastidial and cytosolic isoforms. 2. Loss of AtPPA-AT abolishes female transmission and causes seed lethality. 3. Plastidial, but not cytosolic, PPA-AT is sufficient to rescue the mutant.
